A deep-sea welder repairing an undersea pipeline discovers the weld seams are healing themselves overnight. 3000 words, third person limited, past tense.
A forensic linguist analyzing ransom notes realizes all seven notes from different cases were written by the same person — her recently deceased mother. 3000 words, third person limited, past tense.
A retired astronaut running a laundromat in rural New Mexico keeps finding moon dust in the lint traps. 3000 words, third person limited, past tense.

A night-shift baker discovers that bread left to proof overnight in her shop develops patterns in the crust that match satellite photos of a city that doesn't exist. 3000 words, first person, past tense.
A building demolition specialist is hired to bring down a condemned hospital, but every controlled detonation makes the building taller. 3000 words, third person limited, past tense.

A cartographer updating county maps discovers a road that appears on every historical map but that no GPS, satellite image, or survey has ever detected. She drives out to find it. 3000 words, third person limited, past tense.

A veterinarian treating livestock on an isolated ranch realizes the animals are evolving — visibly, generationally, over weeks instead of millennia. The rancher doesn't see a problem. 3000 words, third person limited, past tense.

A prison librarian notices that books checked out by one particular inmate come back with additional chapters that weren't in the original. The chapters are better than the originals. 3000 words, third person limited, past tense.

An insurance claims adjuster is assigned to assess damage from an earthquake that seismographs say never happened, in a town that insists it did. Every piece of physical evidence supports the town. 3000 words, third person limited, past tense.

A hospice nurse discovers that her patients' final words, when transcribed and read in sequence across a year of deaths, form a coherent technical document. 3000 words, third person limited, past tense.
